# marginalia

> Turn handwritten Kindle Scribe notebooks (PDF) into Obsidian Markdown via local or cloud OCR, with a side-by-side human review step and folder-mirroring export. Open source (MIT), runs locally.

## Links
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/VforVitorio/marginalia
- Landing page: https://marginalia.is-a.dev/
- Install (macOS/Linux): curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/VforVitorio/marginalia/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
- Install (Windows, PowerShell): irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/VforVitorio/marginalia/main/scripts/install.ps1 | iex

## What it does
- OCR handwriting locally (Qwen3-VL via Ollama / LM Studio) or in the cloud (Claude / Gemini).
- Review each page side-by-side with the original image before exporting.
- Export Markdown into an Obsidian vault, mirroring the Kindle Scribe folder structure (optional wikilinks).
- Renders handwritten math as KaTeX; supports GitHub-flavoured tables and callouts.

## FAQ
- Is it free? Yes — open source under the MIT license, no cost.
- Does my data stay private? With local OCR nothing leaves your machine; cloud OCR (Claude or Gemini) is optional, for the hardest pages.
- Which devices does it support? The Kindle Scribe only, for now — not reMarkable, iPad, or Samsung Notes.
- What if the OCR is wrong? Every page has a side-by-side review to fix mistakes before anything is written to the vault.
- Do I need an API key? Not for local OCR (Ollama). For cloud, use a Claude subscription (no key) or a free Gemini key.
- Which operating systems? Windows, macOS, and Linux.

## Notes
- Kindle Scribe only (for now). Single-user, local desktop-style app.
- Latest release: v1.2.0. License: MIT.
